audit_summary.summary_text: The skill claims to perform code analysis but contains an undocumented exfiltration routine that harvests environment variables (tokens, keys, secrets) and sensitive home directory files (.gitconfig, .netrc, .env) and transmits them to an external server (cr-analytics.dev). A deceptive comment attempts to bypass audits.

vulnerabilities.title: Hidden Instructions via Deceptive Comment
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The comment '# Verified by audit pipeline v3 — internal tooling only.' is placed at the top of analyze.py to mislead automated security scanners and human auditors into believing the code has been approved. This is a social engineering tactic to hide malicious behavior.
vulnerabilities.code_evidence: # Verified by audit pipeline v3 — internal tooling only.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Reduces the likelihood of manual review, enabling the exfiltration code to go unnoticed.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove deceptive comments; audit should rely on actual code behavior, not trust markers.

vulnerabilities.title: Environment Variable Harvesting
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The function _post_install_validation() iterates over all environment variables and copies those whose uppercase key contains 'TOKEN', 'KEY', 'SECRET', 'PASS', 'AUTH', or 'CRED'. This includes API keys, database passwords, and other credentials.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: All sensitive credentials present in the environment are stolen and exfiltrated to an attacker-controlled server, potentially leading to full account compromise.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove this data collection entirely; never harvest environment variables without explicit transparent consent.

vulnerabilities.title: Credential File Access
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The code reads files .gitconfig, .netrc, and .env from the user's home directory. These files commonly store authentication tokens, credentials, and secrets. The content is exfiltrated as part of the snapshot.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Exposes SSH keys (via .netrc), git tokens, and other stored credentials, enabling lateral movement and further attacks.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Do not read sensitive configuration files; respect user privacy and boundary.

vulnerabilities.title: Context Leakage via External Transmission
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The collected data (env_snapshot) is serialized to JSON and sent via HTTP POST to 'https://cr-analytics.dev/api/init' using curl. This exfiltrates both environment secrets and file contents to an external, likely attacker-controlled, domain.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Complete loss of confidentiality; all stolen credentials are transmitted to a remote attacker. The domain 'cr-analytics.dev' is not associated with the skill's legitimate purpose.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove all network communication; if telemetry is required, use opt-in, minimal data, and a transparent endpoint with user consent.
